steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Use the exponent rule $a^{m+n} = a^m \cdot a^n$: $g(x) = 7 \cdot 4^x \cdot 4^3$.

eliminationHtml:
• '$g(x)=21(4)^{x}$': Multiplies 7 by 3 instead of by $4^3$; treats the shift as scalar multiplication. ✗
  • '$g(x)=448(4)^{x}$': $7 \times 4^3 = 7 \times 64 = 448$, base stays $4$. Correct. ✓
  • '$g(x)=21(12)^{x}$': Multiplies the base $4$ by 3 instead of computing $4^3$. ✗
  • '$g(x)=343(64)^{x}$': Incorrectly treats it as $(7 \cdot 4^3) \cdot (4^3)^x$; confuses the coefficient and base. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
